overview.title: AI-Powered Development Resources
overview.description: Master AI tools, prompt engineering, and automation to build software faster with ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, Claude, and more

resources.name: GitHub Copilot
resources.shortDesc: AI pair programmer that suggests code and entire functions in real-time
resources.fullDescription: GitHub Copilot is an AI-powered code completion tool that uses OpenAI's Codex model to suggest code and entire functions in real-time. It integrates directly into your IDE (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim) and can understand context from your codebase to provide relevant suggestions. Trained on billions of lines of public code, it supports dozens of languages and frameworks.

specifications.Free Tier: Free for verified students and open-source maintainers
specifications.Supported IDEs: VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Visual Studio
specifications.Languages: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Ruby, Go, C++, C#, and 50+ more

features.Code Completion: Multi-line code suggestions as you type

bestPractices: Write descriptive comments before code blocks for better suggestions
Review all AI-generated code before accepting
Use Tab to accept, Alt+] to cycle through alternatives
Test edge cases - AI may miss error handling
Combine with code review tools for security
Train yourself to recognize hallucinations (fake APIs/methods)

codeSnippet.title: Example: Function from Comment

resources.name: ChatGPT for Code Generation
resources.shortDesc: Conversational AI for code generation, debugging, and learning with GPT-4 Turbo
resources.fullDescription: ChatGPT (GPT-4 Turbo and GPT-4o) is a powerful conversational AI that excels at code generation, debugging, refactoring, and explaining complex concepts. Unlike in-IDE assistants, it provides a chat interface where you can iteratively refine code, ask follow-up questions, and explore different approaches. With the API, you can integrate it into your own tools and workflows.

specifications.Pricing (Plus): $20/month for unlimited GPT-4o, limited GPT-4 Turbo
specifications.Rate Limits (Plus): 80 messages per 3 hours (GPT-4), 40 messages per 3 hours (GPT-4 Turbo)
specifications.Features: Code interpreter, file uploads, web browsing, DALL-E integration

features.Debugging: Paste error messages for detailed explanations
features.Code Review: Get feedback on code quality and best practices
features.Documentation: Generate README files, API docs, comments
features.Learning: Explain concepts, algorithms, and design patterns
features.Architecture Design: Plan system architecture and database schemas

useCases: Debugging complex error messages and stack traces
Learning new frameworks with guided tutorials
Generating SQL queries from natural language
Converting legacy code to modern frameworks
Creating regex patterns and validation logic
bestPractices: Provide context: language, framework, requirements
Ask for explanations along with code
Request multiple approaches for comparison
Verify AI-generated code with tests
Use Code Interpreter for data analysis and debugging
Break complex requests into smaller steps
Specify versions (e.g., 'React 18 with TypeScript')
Always review security implications

promptTemplates.Code Generation: Create a [component/function] in [language] that [does X]. Use [specific libraries/patterns]. Include error handling and TypeScript types.
promptTemplates.Debugging: I'm getting this error: [error message]. Here's my code: [code]. What's wrong and how do I fix it?
promptTemplates.Refactoring: Refactor this code to be more [readable/performant/maintainable]: [code]. Explain the improvements.
promptTemplates.Architecture: Design a [type] system with [requirements]. Include database schema, API endpoints, and tech stack recommendations.

codeSnippet.title: Example Prompt & Response

resources.name: Claude AI by Anthropic
resources.shortDesc: Advanced AI assistant with 200K context window for analyzing entire codebases
resources.fullDescription: Claude (Claude 3 Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) by Anthropic is a powerful AI assistant optimized for long-form content, code analysis, and technical writing. With a massive 200K token context window, Claude can analyze entire codebases, review multiple files at once, and maintain context across long conversations. Known for being more cautious and accurate than GPT-4 in code generation.

features.Code Generation: Thoughtful, well-structured code with fewer errors
features.Code Review: Detailed code review with security focus

useCases: Migrating large codebases to new frameworks
Generating test suites for large modules
bestPractices: Upload all relevant files for maximum context
Ask Claude to think step-by-step for complex problems
Request security and performance considerations
Use for refactoring large functions (better than GPT-4)
Leverage 200K context for cross-file analysis
Ask for trade-offs between different approaches
Use Haiku for fast, simple queries to save costs
Use Opus for critical, complex code generation

codeSnippet.title: Example: Multi-file Refactoring Request

resources.name: Cursor - AI-Native Code Editor
resources.shortDesc: AI-first code editor built on VS Code with inline editing and codebase chat
resources.fullDescription: Cursor is an AI-native code editor (fork of VS Code) that integrates AI deeply into the development workflow. Unlike plugins, AI is built into every aspect: inline editing, codebase-wide Q&A, multi-file edits, and automatic bug fixes. Supports GPT-4, Claude, and custom models. Features 'Cmd+K' for AI commands anywhere in your code and 'Cmd+L' for chatting about your entire codebase.

specifications.Pricing: $20/month (includes GPT-4 and Claude), Free tier with GPT-3.5
specifications.Models: GPT-4, GPT-4 Turbo, Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet, custom models
specifications.Context: Entire codebase indexed for AI queries
specifications.Offline Mode: Editor works offline, AI requires internet
specifications.Privacy: SOC 2 certified, code never used for training

features.Cmd+K: Inline AI editing - select code and ask for changes
features.Cmd+L: Chat with your codebase - ask questions about any file
features.Multi-file Edit: Apply changes across multiple files at once
features.Auto-debug: AI analyzes errors and suggests fixes
features.Codebase Indexing: AI understands your entire project context
features.Model Selection: Switch between GPT-4, Claude, or custom models

useCases: Refactoring large sections of code inline
Applying consistent changes across many files
Debugging with AI that sees your entire project
Code reviews with AI that knows your codebase
bestPractices: Use Cmd+K for quick inline edits, Cmd+L for discussions
Index your codebase for better AI understanding
Review multi-file edits in diff view before accepting
Use '@' to reference specific files in chat
Switch to Claude for complex refactoring (better than GPT-4)
Use 'Apply to multiple files' for project-wide changes
Disable AI in .cursorignore for sensitive files
Use Fast mode (GPT-3.5) for simple queries to save credits

keyboardShortcuts.Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+L: Open chat with current file context

codeSnippet.title: Example: Inline Edit with Cmd+K

resources.name: Tabnine AI Code Completion
resources.shortDesc: Privacy-focused AI code assistant with on-premise deployment options
resources.fullDescription: Tabnine is an AI code completion tool that prioritizes privacy and security. Unlike cloud-only solutions, Tabnine offers on-premise deployment and can be trained on your private codebase. Supports all major IDEs and programming languages. Features both whole-line and full-function completions. Offers a generous free tier and is popular with enterprises due to GDPR/SOC 2 compliance.

specifications.Free Tier: Short code completions, limited AI model
specifications.Privacy: On-premise deployment available, never trains on your code
specifications.Supported IDEs: VS Code, IntelliJ, PyCharm, Android Studio, 15+ more

features.Team Learning: Learns from your team's code patterns
features.Chat: AI chat for code generation and Q&A (Pro)

useCases: Teams with strict data privacy requirements
Training AI on proprietary codebase patterns
Developers wanting a free Copilot alternative
Custom model fine-tuning for specific domains
bestPractices: Use Team plan to share learned patterns across team
Configure completion delay to reduce noise
Enable semantic completion for better context
Use Enterprise plan for sensitive codebases
Combine with pre-commit hooks for code quality
Train private model on your coding standards
Disable for pair programming sessions
Review suggestions for security vulnerabilities

vsGithubCopilot.Privacy: Tabnine: On-premise option | Copilot: Cloud only
vsGithubCopilot.Training: Tabnine: Can train on your code | Copilot: Generic training
vsGithubCopilot.Enterprise: Tabnine: Strong compliance | Copilot: Business tier available

codeSnippet.title: Example: Tabnine Completion

resources.name: Codeium - Free AI Code Acceleration
resources.shortDesc: Forever-free AI code completion and chat for individuals with unlimited usage
resources.fullDescription: Codeium is a free AI-powered code acceleration toolkit offering autocomplete, chat, and search capabilities. Built by Exafunction, it's completely free for individual developers with no usage limits. Supports 70+ languages and integrates with 40+ IDEs. Features include multi-line completions, natural language to code, in-editor AI chat, and codebase-aware suggestions. A great free alternative to GitHub Copilot.

specifications.Pricing: FREE forever for individuals, $12/month Teams, Custom Enterprise
specifications.Supported IDEs: VS Code, JetBrains, Vim/Neovim, Jupyter, 40+ more
specifications.Privacy: Code encrypted in transit, not used for training

features.Autocomplete: Multi-line code suggestions as you type
features.Chat: In-editor AI chat (like ChatGPT in your IDE)
features.Unlimited: No token limits or usage caps (individual plan)

useCases: Students learning to code (better than paid alternatives)
Teams wanting to try AI coding before committing
Anyone wanting free unlimited AI assistance
bestPractices: Use Chat for complex questions, Autocomplete for speed
Reference files with @filename in Chat
Leverage codebase context - it learns your patterns
Use Command K for quick inline edits
Enable 'Diff View' to review suggestions
Customize suggestion frequency in settings
Use for learning - it's free and unlimited
Combine with Cursor for best free setup

whyFree.explanation: Codeium is free for individuals to drive adoption and compete with GitHub Copilot. They monetize through Teams and Enterprise plans with additional features (analytics, admin controls, on-premise deployment). Their strategy focuses on freemium growth.
whyFree.limitations: Individual plan has all core features - no artificial limits. Teams plan adds collaboration features, usage analytics, and priority support. Enterprise adds on-premise deployment and custom models.

codeSnippet.title: Example: Codeium Autocomplete
